---
title: "Quarto 6 — Parameterized Reports"
subtitle: "Generate many reports from one source"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Goals — 25 minutes

::: {.incremental}
- Define parameters in YAML
- Use parameters in code and prose
- Render the same `.qmd` with different parameters
- Batch-generate reports across chemicals, sites, years
:::

# The problem {.section-divider}

## Six reports, same analysis

::: {.incremental}
- PFOA at Research Triangle Park, 2024 ✓
- PFOS at Research Triangle Park, 2024
- PFOA at Durham, 2024
- PFOA at Chapel Hill, 2024
- PFOS at all three sites, 2024
- PFOA in 2023 (comparison)
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Six duplicate files** — or **one parameterized template**.
:::

# Defining parameters {.section-divider}

## In the YAML

```yaml
---
title: "Biomonitoring Report"
format: html
params:
  chemical: "PFOA"
  threshold: 4.0
  study_year: 2024
  site: "Research Triangle Park"
---
```

::: {.fragment}
Strings quote, numbers don't, booleans lowercase, `null` is null.
:::

# Using parameters {.section-divider}

## In prose

```markdown
This report analyzes **`r params$chemical`**
data from **`r params$study_year`** at
**`r params$site`** using a threshold of
**`r params$threshold` ng/mL**.
```

## In code chunks

```{.r}
exposure_data |>
  filter(site == params$site) |>
  summarise(
    n_above = sum(concentration > params$threshold),
    pct = round(n_above / n() * 100, 1)
  )
```

## Dynamic captions

```yaml
#| fig-cap: !expr paste("Distribution of",
#|   params$chemical, "at", params$site)
```

::: {.fragment}
`!expr` evaluates R at render time. Caption updates with parameters.
:::

# Re-rendering {.section-divider}

## From R

```{.r}
quarto::quarto_render(
  "06_parameterized_reports.qmd",
  execute_params = list(
    chemical = "PFOS",
    threshold = 6.0,
    site = "Durham"
  ),
  output_file = "report_PFOS_Durham.html"
)
```

## From the command line

```bash
quarto render 06_parameterized_reports.qmd \
  -P chemical:PFOS \
  -P threshold:6.0 \
  -P site:Durham
```

::: {.fragment}
The `-P` flag is the Quarto improvement over R Markdown — much more scriptable.
:::

## "Render with Parameters" button

In RStudio/Posit Cloud's IDE:

::: {.incremental}
- Click the dropdown arrow next to **Render**
- Select **"Render with Parameters..."**
- Fill in values interactively
- Useful for one-off renders without writing code
:::

# Batch generation {.section-divider}

## The pattern

```{.r}
chemicals <- c("PFOA", "PFOS", "PFNA", "BPA")
sites <- c("RTP", "Durham", "Chapel Hill")
thresholds <- list(PFOA = 4.0, PFOS = 6.0,
                   PFNA = 2.0, BPA = 5.0)

for (chem in chemicals) {
  for (site in sites) {
    quarto::quarto_render(
      "06_parameterized_reports.qmd",
      execute_params = list(
        chemical = chem,
        site = site,
        threshold = thresholds[[chem]]
      ),
      output_file = paste0("report_",
        chem, "_", site, ".html")
    )
  }
}
```

::: {.fragment}
**12 reports** from one source. Add a chemical, re-run, get more.
:::

# When this pattern shines {.section-divider}

## Earns its keep when

::: {.incremental}
- A **template** applies to many cases
- Analysis is **identical**; only inputs change
- Reports regenerate **regularly** as data arrives
:::

::: {.fragment}
NIEHS use cases: annual chemical summaries, multi-site monitoring, sub-population analyses, sensitivity sweeps across thresholds.
:::

# R Markdown vs Quarto {.section-divider}

## Same idea, better plumbing

| Aspect | R Markdown | Quarto |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| YAML | `params:` | `params:` |
| Access | `params$x` | `params$x` |
| Render R | `rmarkdown::render(params=...)` | `quarto::quarto_render(execute_params=...)` |
| Render CLI | not native | `quarto render -P key:value` |
| Dynamic captions | `fig.cap = paste(...)` | `!expr paste(...)` |

# Hands-on {.section-divider}

## Notebook Quarto 6

::: {.callout-tip}
Open `afternoon_quarto/06_parameterized_reports.qmd`. Change YAML parameters, re-render, observe.

Exercises 6.1 and 6.2.
:::

25 min. Then projects + themes + publishing.
